cognates.correspondence_path: Ar س ↔ He שׁ (*š) — standard reflex of *š; note Arabic t-t metathesis/assimilation in root origin *š-d-θ
Syc ܫ ↔ He שׁ (*š); Syc ת ↔ He שׁ (*θ) — reflects original *š-d-θ root structure

caveats: v0 prototype: this is an LLM-generated analysis, not a lexicon lookup. Results may be incomplete or incorrect, especially for Syriac, Tigrinya, and rare forms. Verify against Wiktionary or a scholarly lexicon before citing.
The numeral 'six' originates from a proto-root *š-d-θ. The final *θ reflects differently across branches, often assimilating to geminate forms in later stages as seen in Hebrew שש and Arabic ستة.
The Hebrew form 'שש' is technically a geminate realization of the underlying root *š-d-θ; mapping it to only two slots (שׁ-שׁ) is a surface-level simplification.
